data.title: Bulls and bears hash it out on CAVA Group ahead of earnings
data.description:
CAVA Group (CAVA) heads into its Q1 earnings report with expectations for another strong top-line print, driven by steady traffic and new-unit growth. A key question will be whether the restaurant company can keep that momentum without giving back too much margin to labor and input costs. The company is expected to report revenue of $416M and EPS of $0.17.

Ahead of the report, KeyBanc Capital Markets research analyst Christopher Carril noted that a key debate heading into earnings will be whether CAVA (CAVA) revisits its full-year comparable sales outlook, and especially if the Q1 guidance mark is above the guidance level. "While we think investors are generally expecting guidance to remain conservative, expectations will likely be elevated heading into earnings given the stock’s strong performance to begin the year (+61% YTD, vs. S&P 500 +4%)," highlighted Carril. The firm also pointed to the Glazed Salmon menu introduction and new protein launch as potential catalysts with the earnings report. KeyBanc kept an Overweight rating on CAVA and boosted its price target to $105 from $95.

RBC Capital Markets analyst Logan Reich recently said the firm has higher confidence in upside to CAVA's (CAVA) FY26 same-store sales guidance and boosted estimates with room still for further upside. The firm also has higher confidence in unit growth ramp in FY27 as the manager pipeline grows and margin expansion in FY27 due to investments made in 2026 that are expected to support multiple years of growth. 

On the bearish side of the ledger, Northcoast started off coverage on CAVA Group (CAVA) with a Sell rating due to its valuation, which it warned has gotten ahead of CAVA's (CAVA) own potential. Analyst Jim Sanderson said the biggest risk to Cava's lofty valuation is declining organic sales over time, which would pressure its pricing power. The firm assigned a Street-low price target of $63 to CAVA.

Shares of CAVA Group (CAVA) are up 67% on a year-to-date basis, which has burned some short traders. The short interest on the restaurant stock still stands at 13.8% of the total float.

data.title: Fed chair nominee seeks policy reforms, new framework, new communications
data.description:
Kevin Warsh, President Trump's nominee for Federal Reserve chair, stressed that the central bank must "stay in its lane" to preserve its independence in his opening statement during his confirmation hearing on Tuesday.

The hearing proceeded even as Sen. Thom Tillis (R-NC) vowed to block a vote on the confirmation until the Justice Department drops its investigation of current Fed Chair Jerome Powell.

In his opening statement, released earlier, Warsh stated the importance of Fed independence.

He said, "To the President, Congress, and the nation, I owe my best judgment and most faithful efforts in serving the mission Congress assigned to the Fed, including price stability and full employment."

Warsh accused the Fed of "missing the mark" when it failed to rein in inflation after COVID in 2021 and 2022.

"We need fundamental policy reforms to fix it," he said. In addition, it requires a new inflation framework, needs to use its tools differently, and needs new communications, Warsh said. In particular, he criticized the Fed's use of forward guidance.

Furthermore, he said, "I am committed to ensuring that the conduct of monetary policy remains strictly independent. I am equally committed to working with the Administration and Congress on non-monetary matters that are part of the Fed’s remit. And I commit to accountability in all the Fed’s functions."

Developing… Check back for updates.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: RTX is best positioned after aerospace earnings trio reports: BNP Paribas
data.description:
BNP Paribas analyst Matt Akers on Tuesday struck an upbeat tone on aerospace and defense stocks after reviewing fresh quarterly results from RTX (RTX), Northrop Grumman (NOC) and GE Aerospace (GE), highlighting RTX (RTX) as the clearest winner of the group thanks to stronger guidance and broad-based execution.

In a flash note to clients, Akers said RTX (RTX) delivered a “good result all around,” pointing to earnings beats across Collins Aerospace, Pratt & Whitney and Raytheon. The most meaningful takeaway was a higher outlook for Raytheon, where revenue growth is now expected in the high-single-digit range, above the company’s prior forecast, he said.

Akers added that RTX’s (RTX) 14% organic aftermarket growth should also reassure investors watching the health of commercial aviation demand. He said the quarter could serve as a positive read-across for other aerospace names tied to airline maintenance and fleet activity. BNP Paribas maintained its Outperform rating on RTX with a $235 price target.

Northrop Grumman (NOC) received a more measured response. Akers noted that first-quarter operations were slightly below expectations, particularly on margins and cash flow, even though earnings and revenue topped consensus estimates.

Rather than celebrating the headline beat, Akers signaled that investors would likely focus on management’s conference call for updates on the B-21 bomber program, especially any commentary on acceleration of the strategically important defense platform. BNP Paribas kept its Outperform rating on Northrop (NOC) with an $800 target price.

GE Aerospace (GE) drew perhaps the most nuanced reaction. Akers acknowledged the company posted a strong first-quarter earnings beat, powered largely by its commercial engines and services business, where services revenue surged well above expectations.

Still, he noted that GE (GE) left full-year guidance unchanged and trimmed its outlook for global departures growth, citing weaker trends in the Middle East and softer assumptions elsewhere. While he said the results were likely strong enough to lift the shares in the near term, BNP Paribas continues to rate the stock Underperform with a $290 price target, implying valuation concerns remain.

Taken together, Akers’ post-earnings read suggests investors should favor companies offering fresh upside catalysts rather than just quarterly beats. By that score, RTX appears to have done the most to improve its story, while Northrop still needs program clarity and GE may need stronger guidance to justify its premium valuation.

data.title: Dynatrace, Elastic in focus as Goldman Sachs starts coverage
data.description:
Dynatrace (DT) and Elastic (ESTC) were in focus on Tuesday as Goldman Sachs started coverage on the enterprise software companies.

“We initiate coverage of Dynatrace (DT, Buy, $45 PT) and Elastic (ESTC, Neutral, $50 PT),” analysts at the firm wrote in a note to clients. “Observability is entering a particularly dynamic period, as AI deepens the strategic importance of machine data, and raises the bar for the platforms built to harness it. Compute intensity is scaling rapidly as AI workloads proliferate, workload complexity is expanding the coverage surface area, and the shift from reactive monitoring to autonomous remediation is pulling budget away from fragmented point solutions and toward platforms built to compress signal-to-action.”

For Dynatrace, Goldman Sachs said the company “sits squarely” in the cross current of the aforementioned shift in the observability market. And with a fresh product cycle, and new DPS contract transition, the company's growth should reset into next year and margins should be strong. It also has free cash flow margins of 25%, the analysts noted. 

For Elastic, the platform still has “structural relevance” across search, observability and security, and its execution has improved, but it's not clear yet that cloud growth is durable.

“We still need to see clearer evidence that cloud growth can durably inflect – validating both the AI narrative and reinforcing Elastic’s positioning in an increasingly competitive backdrop – before getting more constructive,” the analysts wrote.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Tuesday: AMZN, LCID, UNH, PBI, and more
data.description:
Stock futures rose slightly on Tuesday, with attention on Kevin Warsh's important Congressional confirmation hearing for the Federal Reserve chair position. Meanwhile, investors remained cautious about the prospects for U.S.-Iran peace talks as a temporary ceasefire deadline approached.

Here are some of Tuesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Lucid Group (LCID) +12% - Shares rose after Uber Technologies ((UBER)) disclosed an 11.5% passive stake (~37.75M shares) via its subsidiary, alongside deeper strategic ties. The investment is part of Lucid’s broader $1.05B capital raise, which includes backing from the Public Investment Fund. Uber also boosted its commitment to purchase at least 35,000 Lucid vehicles for its planned global robotaxi network and increased its total investment in the EV maker to $500M, reinforcing a long-term partnership in autonomous mobility.
  • Pitney Bowes (PBI) +11% - Shares gained after issuing upbeat preliminary Q1 results and raising full-year guidance, signaling improving operational momentum. Adjusted EPS came in at ~$0.47 (vs. $0.33 Y/Y), and EBIT rose to ~$130M, while free cash flow swung to a positive ~$44M from a prior outflow. Despite a 3% Y/Y revenue decline to ~$477M, the print topped consensus and showed moderating pressure. The company lifted FY revenue, EBIT, EPS, and free cash flow outlooks, reinforcing confidence in margin expansion and cash generation.
  • UnitedHealth Group (UNH) +9% - Shares surged after delivering better-than-expected Q1 2026 results and raising its full-year outlook, reinforcing its position as a key bellwether for the earnings season. The company now expects non-GAAP EPS of more than $18.25, ahead of both prior guidance and consensus estimates, signaling strong operational momentum. In addition, UnitedHealth announced a $2B share buyback program through Q2 2026, further boosting investor confidence alongside continued focus on cost efficiency and healthcare system modernization.
  • Amazon.com (AMZN) +2% - Shares gained after announcing a deeper AI push with Anthropic, committing an additional $5B investment with plans to scale up to $20B over time tied to milestones. The expanded partnership cements AWS as Anthropic’s primary cloud and training provider. The AI firm is set to spend over $100B on AWS infrastructure over the next decade, including Trainium chips and Graviton compute, alongside securing up to 5GW of capacity. The deal also integrates Anthropic’s Claude platform directly into AWS, strengthening Amazon’s position in the generative AI race and driving investor optimism.

Biggest stock losers

  • Tractor Supply Company (TSCO) -8% - Shares plunged after missing Q1 expectations, as softer-than-expected comparable sales offset otherwise steady top-line growth. Revenue rose 3.6% to $3.59B, but comp sales increased just 0.5%, well below the 1.6% consensus, with lower transaction volumes weighing on performance. Margins remained stable, though operating income fell 6.3% and EPS declined to $0.31, missing estimates. While management highlighted strength in new store openings and double-digit digital growth, the weak comp trajectory drove the negative reaction, even as full-year guidance came broadly in line with expectations.
  • AXT (AXTI) -5% - Shares dipped after pricing a ~$550M public offering at $64.25 per share, a steep discount to its prior close of $78.76, pressuring the stock. The deal includes a 30-day option for underwriters to purchase additional shares, potentially lifting proceeds to ~$632.5M. The company plans to use the funds to expand indium phosphide substrate capacity at its Beijing Tongmei subsidiary, alongside R&D and general corporate purposes.
  • GE Aerospace (GE) -4% - Shares fell despite a strong Q1 beat, as investors focused on unchanged full-year guidance that still trails consensus expectations. Revenue came in at $12.39B and adjusted EPS at $1.86, both ahead of estimates, reflecting solid operational momentum. However, the company maintained its 2026 EPS outlook of $7.10–$7.40 (vs. ~$7.46 consensus) and free cash flow of $8.0B–$8.4B, with no formal raise despite a strong start. The muted outlook disappointed investors who were expecting an upward revision, driving the stock lower.
  • Alaska Air Group (ALK) -3% - Shares slid despite strong Q1 results after suspending its FY2026 outlook due to limited earnings visibility driven by fuel price volatility and geopolitical uncertainty. The airline flagged a wide range of potential outcomes for Q2, opting to withhold EPS guidance while indicating capacity growth of ~1% and high single-digit unit revenue gains, though partially offset by weather-related disruptions in Hawai’i. Rising fuel costs, expected to average ~$4.50 per gallon, are projected to add ~$600M in expenses (~$3.60 EPS headwind), while near-term unit cost pressures from capacity adjustments, training, and one-off items are expected to ease in the second half of the year.

data.title: Moderna granted EU nod for combined COVID-19 and flu vaccine
data.description:
• Moderna (MRNA) announced on Tuesday that the European Commission issued marketing authorization for mCOMBRIAX (mRNA-1083), its messenger RNA-based vaccine designed to target both COVID-19 and influenza.
  • The agency has cleared the single-dose shot to prevent influenza disease and COVID-19 caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus in people aged 50 years and older, the Cambridge, Massachusetts-based biotech said in a statement.
  • The approval, which takes effect across all 27 EU member states, as well as Iceland, Liechtenstein, and Norway, comes after an expert panel of the EU drug regulator European Medicines Agency ((EMA)) recommended the vaccine in February.
  • The company added that it will engage national healthcare officials as it plans to launch mCOMBRIAX in the EU, a process it said will depend on local regulatory and access procedures.

data.title: Warsh testimony: Traders bet on themes around inflation, rates, and AI
data.description:
Kevin Warsh, nominated by Donald Trump to lead the Federal Reserve, is set to testify before the Senate Banking Committee, with markets closely watching for policy signals.

Investors are focused less on confirmation odds and more on the substance of Warsh’s remarks, particularly around inflation trends, the timing of potential rate cuts, and the economic implications of artificial intelligence. Prediction market platform Kalshi indicates traders expect these themes to dominate the hearing. 

The testimony could provide insight into how Warsh might steer monetary policy if confirmed, offering markets a clearer sense of the Federal Reserve’s future direction. Outlined below are what traders on Kalshi are pricing in to what Warsh will say during the hearing: 

  • Inflation — 98% chance.
  • Trump — 98% chance.
  • Wife — 96% chance.
  • 2008 / Financial Crisis — 94% chance.
  • Powell — 94% chance.
  • AI / Artificial Intelligence — 89% chance.
  • Crypto / Cryptocurrency / Bitcoin — 86% chance. 
  • Rate Cut / Cut Rate — 85% chance. 
  • Tariff — 84% chance.
  • Oil — 84% chance. 
  • Bessent — 49% chance. 
  • Iran — 49% chance.
  • Estee Lauder — 46% chance. 
  • Cook — 30% chance.
  • Renovation — 26% chance. 
  • Epstein — 18% chance.
  • Judy Shelton — 4% chance. 

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: Intel's former CEO joins board of Australian semiconductor startup Syenta
data.description:
Intel's (INTC) former CEO Pat Gelsinger is joining the board of directors for Syenta, an Australia-based semiconductor company focused on building advanced packaging technology for artificial intelligence systems.

Syenta recently completed a Series A funding round of $26M. The round was led by Playground Global, where Gelsinger serves as a general partner. Australia's National Reconstruction Fund co-led with participation from Investible, Salus Ventures, Jelix Ventures, and Wollemi Capital. The funds are intended to help Syenta move towards commercialization.

The company, headquartered in Sydney, has developed a technology called Localized Electrochemical Manufacturing, which enables high-density chip-to-chip interconnects within existing semiconductor manufacturing infrastructure. This is supposed to shorten the advanced packaging process for AI systems and help alleviate supply chain constraints across the industry. The technology is also intended to improve the efficiency of how chips connect and communicate.

Syenta said it can lead to a 40% reduction in process steps, requires no redesign for existing fabrication infrastructure, and improves the bandwidth between chips.

"This is a new way of building high-performance systems at unprecedented scale and power, particularly as AI workloads continue to grow," Gelsinger said. "AI's next scaling challenge isn’t just compute—it's how chips connect. Syenta is addressing a fundamental constraint in advanced packaging, and that has the potential to reshape how future systems are built and scaled."

As part of its commercialization strategy, Syenta is establishing an office in Arizona, located near facilities operated by Intel and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM).

"There's this connected part between cheap chiplets that currently are not keeping up with the demand in AI compute," said Syenta co-founder and CEO Jeka Viktorova in an interview last year with Forbes Australia. "Our method accelerates this connection, and we're able to build the parts that connect it to chiplets much faster than conventional technology."

Viktorova said their solution will help overcome a bottleneck in advanced packaging and make AI and quantum chips more efficient.

data.title: Watch these stocks after the retail sales report came in strong
data.description:
Retail sales shot up in March as tax refunds boosted consumer spending, with an easy comparison to a year ago when tariff concerns weighed. While the March increase in retail sales was led by a record jump in spending on gas, almost every category notched an increase. 

Retail sales climbed 1.7% M/M to $752.1B in March, topping the +1.4% consensus and accelerating from the +0.7% pace in February, according to advance data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Tuesday. Better yet, the February increase in retail sales was revised up from the +0.6% rate initially reported.

The electronics & appliance stores category (+0.9% M/M, +5.2% Y/Y) had a strong month, which bodes well for both Best Buy (BBY) and GameStop (GME).

The nonstore retailers category (+1.0% M/M, +10.1% Y/Y) shined once again. Amazon (AMZN) is the most important name in the category, although the strong showing could also be a positive for Bath & Body Works (BBWI), Etsy (ETSY), and Wayfair (W).

The building material and garden equipment category (+0.7% M/M, +2.6% Y/Y) also had a strong month in a read that is usually positive for Home Depot (HD), Lowe's (LOW), Tractor Supply (TSCO), Floor & Decor (FND), and Fastenal (FAST).

"Higher gasoline prices amid the Iran war drove the headline gains in March retail sales. Even with consumers forced to allocate more of their budgets to energy, overall demand held up better than expected, likely reflecting larger tax refunds and continued resilience among higher-income households. The picture is less sanguine once we account for inflation, however," updated the Bloomberg economics team.

"Higher gas prices are taking a bite, but the damage is limited. While these numbers are good news for the economy overall, they don’t necessarily support rate cuts by the Federal Reserve," updated David Russell, global head of market strategy at TradeStation.

data.title: Wall Street rises as investors renew hopes of a U.S.-Iran truce, Warsh faces confirmation hearing
data.description:
U.S. stocks were higher on Tuesday as investors hope for a peace deal between Iran and the U.S. while former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh started his confirmation hearings in the Senate.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.3%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.4%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.7%.

Over in the bond market, the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 3 basis points higher at 4.28%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose 4 basis points to 3.77%.

U.S. President Donald Trump told CNBC that he expects the U.S. to “end up with a great deal” with Iran to end the war. He also said he does not expect to extend the ongoing ceasefire, which will expire on Wednesday. “I think they have no choice,” he said.

Vice President JD Vance, along with the U.S. delegation, is expected to land in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Wednesday for another round of talks, CNN reported.

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) were lower at $87, while Brent (CO1:COM) also slid to $95 per barrel.

Kevin Warsh, the former Fed governor whom President Donald Trump nominated as Federal Reserve chair, will face a barrage of questions on Tuesday during his confirmation hearings in the Senate. The confirmation hearing starts at 10 a.m. ET.

On the U.S. economy, retail sales jumped more than expected in March.

Pending home sales jumped 1.5% month-over-month in March, higher than consensus, the National Association of Realtors said. Moreover, February business inventories rose +0.4% MoM to $2,686.8B vs. +0.2% consensus, according to the U.S. Census Bureau.
